title: Commercial Opportunity Sub-Agent Output (v1)
description: Structured output from the commercial_opportunity sub-agent runner (v4 Phase 2b). Captures the commercial half of the investment thesis: TAM, standard of care + its side-effect burden, unmet-need severity, regulatory incentives. Stage 1 weaves this into the cited prose alongside the regulatory thesis. Hard-validated before return; failure → failed_reactor_events with source='sub_agent.commercial_opportunity'.

low_usd.description: Lower bound of total addressable market in USD (annual peak). Null only if no defensible estimate is possible.

high_usd.description: Upper bound of TAM in USD.

is_inferred.description: True whenever the range isn't backed by a primary market-research source — usually true. Honesty norm: prefer is_inferred=true over a wrong-precise number.

rationale.description: Patient-population × annual-cost × penetration reasoning, plus any primary sources cited.

primary_source_url.description: Optional market-research URL backing the range. Null when is_inferred=true and no source was found.

is_currently_marketed.description: False for withdrawn drugs that still anchor the historical SoC narrative.

primary_source_url.description: FDA label or openFDA approval record URL.

soc_limitations.description: Short strings describing where SoC falls short (efficacy ceiling, durability, contraindications, dosing burden, etc.). Each item is one limitation, not a paragraph.

drug.description: Must match a `drug` value from standard_of_care[].

ae_term.description: Adverse event term as it appears in the FDA label adverse_reactions section.

frequency_band.description: Use 'unknown' rather than inventing percentages when the label is silent.

drives_discontinuation.description: True when the AE is documented as a leading cause of treatment discontinuation.

unmet_need_severity_1_5.description: 5 = no adequate therapy / mortality-driving. 4 = poor options with major AE burden. 3 = adequate options but improvement clearly valuable. 2 = mild incremental. 1 = crowded category with strong options.

unmet_need_rationale.description: Optional one-paragraph rationale for the severity score; grounds the integer.

regulatory_incentives.description: FDA designations the asset has OR is likely to receive. Use ['none'] when no designations apply — never empty. Inflated claims will reduce evidence_quality at Stage 1; the orchestrator cross-checks against extracted_facts.regulatory_designations.

competitive_landscape_summary.description: One concise paragraph (~2000 chars target, 5000 hard cap) summarising the competitive context + differentiation read. Detailed competitor enumeration is the competitive sub-agent's job; this is commercial context only.

mcap_to_peak_revenue_ratio.description: Sponsor market cap / peak-revenue estimate from TAM × realistic-penetration. Null when sponsor is private OR sizing is too speculative. v0 runner does not call polygon get_market_cap (see runner docstring); expect mostly null on v0.

sourcing_completeness_pct.description: Fraction of schema fields grounded in retrieved evidence vs prior-knowledge inference. Drives Stage 1 evidence_quality.

sourcing_completeness_rationale.description: Optional one-paragraph rationale for the sourcing_completeness_pct score; counts which fields were grounded vs inferred. Sonnet emits this by pattern-match with tam_estimate.rationale / unmet_need_rationale even when the prompt only asks for the number — accept it rather than DLQ the call.

partial_output.description: True if budget was exhausted before completion.
